concerns.text: The plan relies on ClinicalTrials.gov and OSF APIs to extract specific numeric outcome data (pre/post means, SDs, sample sizes) for Hedges' g calculation. ClinicalTrials.gov API (v2) primarily returns registry metadata and high-level outcome summaries, rarely providing the granular raw statistics (means/SDs per arm) required for meta-analysis without manual extraction from linked publications. OSF similarly hosts protocols but not necessarily the final analyzed datasets with the specific variables needed. Relying on these APIs alone to populate the 'Cleaned Study' schema (data-model.md) is a high-risk dataset-variable fit failure; the plan must explicitly account for the likelihood that these sources will yield zero studies with the required numeric fields, necessitating a primary reliance on full-text PDF extraction (FR-009) rather than API metadata.

The plan lists 'Semantic Scholar / arXiv / OpenAlex' as sources for 'full-text PDFs to reconstruct missing statistics'. These repositories are metadata/search engines, not direct hosts of full-text PDFs for clinical trials (which are typically behind paywalls or in journal-specific repositories). The plan assumes these APIs can retrieve the actual PDF content needed for FR-009 reconstruction. This is a dataset access mismatch; the plan needs to cite specific open-access repositories (e.g., PubMed Central, DOAJ) or acknowledge that PDF retrieval will fail for the majority of studies, rendering the reconstruction strategy infeasible for the expected N.

The plan states 'Processing of < 100 studies (expected N < 10 for subgroup analysis)'. While the specific number is deferred, the phrasing 'expected N < 10' is an empirical assertion about the dataset size rather than a citation of a source or a reference to the inclusion criteria. It should be rephrased to 'Processing of studies retrieved from [Source X], with subgroup analysis contingent on N >= 10 per FR-014' to avoid pre-asserting the count.
